editor_note: This week brings a strong focus on building practical AI skills for the workplace, alongside significant developments in how governments control access to advanced AI systems. We also look at new efforts to make AI safer and more transparent, and at how AI is being applied to personalise learning. Together, these stories highlight the growing intersection of education, public policy, and responsible AI use.

items.title: New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
items.summary: OpenAI has launched three new free 'Academy' courses designed to help people build practical AI skills, set up repeatable work routines, and use AI assistants ('agents') in their daily tasks. The courses are aimed at everyday workers rather than technical specialists.
items.why_it_matters: These ready-made courses could support Tamkeen's workforce upskilling efforts by offering accessible, practical AI training for non-technical staff.

items.title: US asks Anthropic to block global access to top AI models: Why it matters - Al Jazeera
items.summary: The US government has reportedly asked AI company Anthropic to restrict access to its most advanced AI models for users outside the country. The move reflects growing concern over who can use powerful AI tools internationally.
items.why_it_matters: Restrictions on advanced AI tools may limit which systems are available to organisations in the UAE and the wider region, affecting future technology choices.

items.title: U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models - The New York Times
items.summary: According to The New York Times, the US has barred foreign nationals from using Anthropic's most advanced AI models. This is part of a broader tightening of access to leading AI technologies along national lines.
items.why_it_matters: Such restrictions signal that access to frontier AI may increasingly depend on geography, which matters for regional planning and partnerships.

items.title: US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera
items.summary: Al Jazeera reports that the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure underscores how governments are exerting more control over cross-border use of advanced AI.
items.why_it_matters: Understanding these access limits helps Tamkeen anticipate which AI tools may or may not be usable for staff and national programmes.

items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced 'Deployment Simulation', a method that predicts how an AI model will behave before it is released, using real conversation data. The aim is to catch problems early and improve safety and testing accuracy.
items.why_it_matters: Better pre-release safety checks support the responsible adoption of AI in public-sector settings where reliability is essential.

items.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
items.summary: Language-learning platform Preply is combining AI tools with human tutors to personalise lessons, including AI-generated lesson summaries and tailored feedback. The approach keeps human teachers central while using AI to enhance learning.
items.why_it_matters: This is a practical example of how AI can strengthen, rather than replace, human-led education and skills development.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.summary: OpenAI is backing the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI content transparency, which helps people identify when content is AI-generated. The effort focuses on standards and tools for tracing the origin of digital content.
items.why_it_matters: Transparency standards for AI content offer a useful reference point as the UAE develops its own approaches to trustworthy and responsible AI.

items.title: Why the US government shut down Anthropic’s latest Claude AI model - The Conversation
items.summary: The Conversation explains the reasons behind the US government's decision to limit access to Anthropic's latest Claude AI model. The article unpacks the security and policy concerns driving these new controls.
items.why_it_matters: It provides helpful context for non-technical staff on why governments are increasingly regulating who can use the most powerful AI systems.

items.text: ● DRAFT — for Policy & Strategy review
AI Pulse — Weekly AI News Digest
Week of 11–17 June 2026

body.text: This week brings a strong focus on building practical AI skills for the workplace, alongside significant developments in how governments control access to advanced AI systems. We also look at new efforts to make AI safer and more transparent, and at how AI is being applied to personalise learning. Together, these stories highlight the growing intersection of education, public policy, and responsible AI use.

items.text: [1. New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work](https://openai.com/index/academy-courses-applying-ai-at-work)

OpenAI has launched three new free 'Academy' courses designed to help people build practical AI skills, set up repeatable work routines, and use AI assistants ('agents') in their daily tasks. The courses are aimed at everyday workers rather than technical specialists.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** These ready-made courses could support Tamkeen's workforce upskilling efforts by offering accessible, practical AI training for non-technical staff.
OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026

[2. US asks Anthropic to block global access to top AI models: Why it matters - Al Jazeera](https://news.google.?oc=5)

The US government has reportedly asked AI company Anthropic to restrict access to its most advanced AI models for users outside the country. The move reflects growing concern over who can use powerful AI tools internationally.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Restrictions on advanced AI tools may limit which systems are available to organisations in the UAE and the wider region, affecting future technology choices.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 14 Jun 2026

[3. U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models - The New York Times](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxPTHRjTk4ySzZxRVVlTnA4LXdBSzZ3RzFEX0puLW1ZTVhxNXRSTmN3ZEVhV0phNWF5YzhfMVhZWGhnR0RlMzhDeUx6RkhuWXRYR0YyTWVTdU1EX3ZZV3l5S3MtRW5LbHczR2VlWDRVSjlVcnRKVTY4Y0ZmZ1V5bWdpb1dVQXBTLTdTMUE?oc=5)

According to The New York Times, the US has barred foreign nationals from using Anthropic's most advanced AI models. This is part of a broader tightening of access to leading AI technologies along national lines.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Such restrictions signal that access to frontier AI may increasingly depend on geography, which matters for regional planning and partnerships.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026

[4. US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera](https://news.google.?oc=5)

Al Jazeera reports that the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure underscores how governments are exerting more control over cross-border use of advanced AI.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Understanding these access limits helps Tamkeen anticipate which AI tools may or may not be usable for staff and national programmes.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026
[5. Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment](https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation)

OpenAI has introduced 'Deployment Simulation', a method that predicts how an AI model will behave before it is released, using real conversation data. The aim is to catch problems early and improve safety and testing accuracy.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Better pre-release safety checks support the responsible adoption of AI in public-sector settings where reliability is essential.
OpenAI · 16 Jun 2026
[6. How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning](https://openai.com/index/preply)

Language-learning platform Preply is combining AI tools with human tutors to personalise lessons, including AI-generated lesson summaries and tailored feedback. The approach keeps human teachers central while using AI to enhance learning.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** This is a practical example of how AI can strengthen, rather than replace, human-led education and skills development.
OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026
[7. Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem](https://openai.com/index/supporting-eu-trustworthy-ai-ecosystem)

OpenAI is backing the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI content transparency, which helps people identify when content is AI-generated. The effort focuses on standards and tools for tracing the origin of digital content.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Transparency standards for AI content offer a useful reference point as the UAE develops its own approaches to trustworthy and responsible AI.
OpenAI · 11 Jun 2026

[8. Why the US government shut down Anthropic’s latest Claude AI model - The Conversation](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiogFBVV95cUxNWUpveEJWVS1OcWh3cDBXVFl3SXAxUUpxemJtNXEtUm9fZWkzNVpRS2wtaU1kZDNIb0pWRkFLdllyNkpSMzNTTXV4a2trWFlNNkN5aGI3RTY5b0VSMm54bjY3a25xUXZ0bmhLUVhPSGhjUXljMWtBNk56ekJ6bEpBRHhibDg4MU1ocWRlbFQtaXltc1h6LXRReTI0d0VWLWdNZ1E?oc=5)

The Conversation explains the reasons behind the US government's decision to limit access to Anthropic's latest Claude AI model. The article unpacks the security and policy concerns driving these new controls.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It provides helpful context for non-technical staff on why governments are increasingly regulating who can use the most powerful AI systems.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 15 Jun 2026
Automated digest curated for Tamkeen by Policy & Strategy.

plain_text:
*** DRAFT — for Policy & Strategy review ***

AI Pulse — Weekly AI News Digest
Week of 11–17 June 2026
============================================================
This week brings a strong focus on building practical AI skills for the workplace, alongside significant developments in how governments control access to advanced AI systems. We also look at new efforts to make AI safer and more transparent, and at how AI is being applied to personalise learning. Together, these stories highlight the growing intersection of education, public policy, and responsible AI use.

1. New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
   https://openai.com/index/academy-courses-applying-ai-at-work
   OpenAI has launched three new free 'Academy' courses designed to help people build practical AI skills, set up repeatable work routines, and use AI assistants ('agents') in their daily tasks. The courses are aimed at everyday workers rather than technical specialists.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: These ready-made courses could support Tamkeen's workforce upskilling efforts by offering accessible, practical AI training for non-technical staff.
   (OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026)

2. US asks Anthropic to block global access to top AI models: Why it matters - Al Jazeera
   https://news.google.?oc=5
   The US government has reportedly asked AI company Anthropic to restrict access to its most advanced AI models for users outside the country. The move reflects growing concern over who can use powerful AI tools internationally.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Restrictions on advanced AI tools may limit which systems are available to organisations in the UAE and the wider region, affecting future technology choices.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 14 Jun 2026)

3. U.S. Bars Foreigners From Using Anthropic’s Most Advanced A.I. Models - The New York Times
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxPTHRjTk4ySzZxRVVlTnA4LXdBSzZ3RzFEX0puLW1ZTVhxNXRSTmN3ZEVhV0phNWF5YzhfMVhZWGhnR0RlMzhDeUx6RkhuWXRYR0YyTWVTdU1EX3ZZV3l5S3MtRW5LbHczR2VlWDRVSjlVcnRKVTY4Y0ZmZ1V5bWdpb1dVQXBTLTdTMUE?oc=5
   According to The New York Times, the US has barred foreign nationals from using Anthropic's most advanced AI models. This is part of a broader tightening of access to leading AI technologies along national lines.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Such restrictions signal that access to frontier AI may increasingly depend on geography, which matters for regional planning and partnerships.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026)

4. US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera
   https://news.google.?oc=5
   Al Jazeera reports that the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure underscores how governments are exerting more control over cross-border use of advanced AI.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Understanding these access limits helps Tamkeen anticipate which AI tools may or may not be usable for staff and national programmes.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026)

5. Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
   https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation
   OpenAI has introduced 'Deployment Simulation', a method that predicts how an AI model will behave before it is released, using real conversation data. The aim is to catch problems early and improve safety and testing accuracy.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Better pre-release safety checks support the responsible adoption of AI in public-sector settings where reliability is essential.
   (OpenAI · 16 Jun 2026)

6. How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
   https://openai.com/index/preply
   Language-learning platform Preply is combining AI tools with human tutors to personalise lessons, including AI-generated lesson summaries and tailored feedback. The approach keeps human teachers central while using AI to enhance learning.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: This is a practical example of how AI can strengthen, rather than replace, human-led education and skills development.
   (OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026)

7. Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
   https://openai.com/index/supporting-eu-trustworthy-ai-ecosystem
   OpenAI is backing the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI content transparency, which helps people identify when content is AI-generated. The effort focuses on standards and tools for tracing the origin of digital content.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Transparency standards for AI content offer a useful reference point as the UAE develops its own approaches to trustworthy and responsible AI.
   (OpenAI · 11 Jun 2026)

8. Why the US government shut down Anthropic’s latest Claude AI model - The Conversation
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiogFBVV95cUxNWUpveEJWVS1OcWh3cDBXVFl3SXAxUUpxemJtNXEtUm9fZWkzNVpRS2wtaU1kZDNIb0pWRkFLdllyNkpSMzNTTXV4a2trWFlNNkN5aGI3RTY5b0VSMm54bjY3a25xUXZ0bmhLUVhPSGhjUXljMWtBNk56ekJ6bEpBRHhibDg4MU1ocWRlbFQtaXltc1h6LXRReTI0d0VWLWdNZ1E?oc=5
   The Conversation explains the reasons behind the US government's decision to limit access to Anthropic's latest Claude AI model. The article unpacks the security and policy concerns driving these new controls.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: It provides helpful context for non-technical staff on why governments are increasingly regulating who can use the most powerful AI systems.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 15 Jun 2026)

------------------------------------------------------------
Automated digest curated for Tamkeen by Policy & Strategy.

[4. US orders Anthropic to disable AI models for all foreign nationals - Al Jazeera](https://news.google.?oc=5)

Al Jazeera reports that the US has ordered Anthropic to disable its AI models for all foreign nationals. The measure underscores how governments are exerting more control over cross-border use of advanced AI.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Understanding these access limits helps Tamkeen anticipate which AI tools may or may not be usable for staff and national programmes.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 13 Jun 2026
[5. Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment](https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation)

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Better pre-release safety checks support the responsible adoption of AI in public-sector settings where reliability is essential.
OpenAI · 16 Jun 2026
[6. How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning](https://openai.com/index/preply)

Language-learning platform Preply is combining AI tools with human tutors to personalise lessons, including AI-generated lesson summaries and tailored feedback. The approach keeps human teachers central while using AI to enhance learning.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** This is a practical example of how AI can strengthen, rather than replace, human-led education and skills development.
OpenAI · 12 Jun 2026
[7. Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem](https://openai.com/index/supporting-eu-trustworthy-ai-ecosystem)

OpenAI is backing the European Union's voluntary Code of Practice on AI content transparency, which helps people identify when content is AI-generated. The effort focuses on standards and tools for tracing the origin of digital content.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Transparency standards for AI content offer a useful reference point as the UAE develops its own approaches to trustworthy and responsible AI.
OpenAI · 11 Jun 2026

[8. Why the US government shut down Anthropic’s latest Claude AI model - The Conversation](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiogFBVV95cUxNWUpveEJWVS1OcWh3cDBXVFl3SXAxUUpxemJtNXEtUm9fZWkzNVpRS2wtaU1kZDNIb0pWRkFLdllyNkpSMzNTTXV4a2trWFlNNkN5aGI3RTY5b0VSMm54bjY3a25xUXZ0bmhLUVhPSGhjUXljMWtBNk56ekJ6bEpBRHhibDg4MU1ocWRlbFQtaXltc1h6LXRReTI0d0VWLWdNZ1E?oc=5)

The Conversation explains the reasons behind the US government's decision to limit access to Anthropic's latest Claude AI model. The article unpacks the security and policy concerns driving these new controls.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It provides helpful context for non-technical staff on why governments are increasingly regulating who can use the most powerful AI systems.
